title: Symmetrized convention for Tr_sigma(M^p B) used before it is defined; shared-spectrum identity implicit

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Where.** Statement of Lemma (Barrier update) [lem:bu], eq. (U), and eq. (total) in Step 4.

**The gap (consistency / faithfulness).** The quantities $\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(M^{-2}B)$ and
$\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(M^{-1}B)$ appear in the *statement* of lem:bu and in the definition of
$U(S,t)$ (eq. (U)) BEFORE the "symmetrized convention"
$\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(AN):=\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(N^{1/2}AN^{1/2})$ is introduced (that definition appears later,
inside the $\Pi_S^\perp$/$\Pi_S$ computation). Since $M^{-2}B$ and $M^{-1}B$ are products of a
symmetric and a PSD matrix and are NOT symmetric in general, these symbols are undefined at
their point of first use unless the symmetrized convention is invoked there.

Moreover, for lem:bu's hypothesis to be the SAME quantity that is bounded by the SMW
computation, one needs the identity
\[
  \operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(M^{p}B) \;=\; \operatorname{Tr}_\sigma\!\big(B^{1/2}M^{p}B^{1/2}\big)
                       \;=\; \operatorname{Tr}_\sigma\!\big(C^\top M^{p}C\big),
  \qquad B = CC^\top,
\]
which holds because $B^{1/2}M^pB^{1/2}$ and $C^\top M^pC$ share the same nonzero spectrum
(the shared-nonzero-spectrum identity for $XY$ vs. $YX$). This identity is what makes the
hypothesis of lem:bu match the $U(S,t)$ used in Step 4 and the SMW term in the proof of
lem:bu; it is used implicitly but never stated.

**Severity.** Expository / faithfulness, not a logical error — I verified numerically that
all the identities hold. But as written, a reader cannot confirm that the symbol in the
lemma statement, the symbol in eq. (U), and the symbol produced by the SMW expansion denote
the same number without the missing convention being applied at first use and the
shared-spectrum identity being stated.

**Fix.** At the first appearance of $\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(M^pB)$ (lemma statement), add: "all
mixed products $\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(AN)$ with $A$ symmetric, $N\succeq0$ are read in the
symmetrized sense $\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(N^{1/2}AN^{1/2})$, and $\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(M^pB)=
\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(C^\top M^pC)$ for $B=CC^\top$ since the two matrices share their nonzero spectra."

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
**Fix applied.** I resolved the convention/identity gap exactly as recommended.

**1. Symmetrized convention at first appearance.** The symbols
$\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(M^{-2}B)$ and $\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(M^{-1}B)$ first appear in the
*statement* of Lemma (Barrier update) [lem:bu]. I added a Convention paragraph immediately
after that statement declaring that **throughout the note** -- explicitly "already in the
statement above, in (U), in (total), and in the proof of [lem:greedy]" -- every mixed product
$\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(A'N)$ with $A'$ symmetric and $N\succeq0$ is read as
$\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(N^{1/2}A'N^{1/2})$, consistent with the ordinary trace via cyclicity.

**2. Shared-nonzero-spectrum identity stated and proved.** In the same paragraph I added
the labeled identity (eq:bu-shared-spectrum):
$\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(M^pB)=\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(B^{1/2}M^pB^{1/2})=\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(C^\top M^pC)$
for $B=CC^\top$, $p\in\{-1,-2\}$, with a self-contained proof: setting $H:=M^{p/2}$,
$J:=B^{1/2}H$, $K:=HC$, one has
$B^{1/2}M^pB^{1/2}=JJ^\top$, $J^\top J=HBH=HCC^\top H=KK^\top$, $K^\top K=C^\top M^pC$,
and the $XY$/$YX$ shared-nonzero-spectrum fact gives equal top-$\sigma$ sums (at most
$\operatorname{rank}(B)\le\sigma$ nonzero eigenvalues, padded with zeros).

**3. First uses now reference the convention.** At eq.\ (U) in Step 4 I inserted a sentence
stating that $\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(M^{-2}\widetilde L_{S,t})$,
$\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(M^{-1}\widetilde L_{S,t})$ are read in the symmetrized sense of the
convention stated with [lem:bu]. In the proof of [lem:greedy] the "scalar hypothesis" already
points to [lem:bu]; the convention there is now well-defined. In Step 4 the in-line
definition was changed from "we adopt" to "we recall ... stated with Lemma [lem:bu]" so it is
no longer presented as a new definition introduced mid-proof. In the proof of [lem:bu] the
opening paragraph now says the convention is "stated with the lemma" (not "introduced in
Step 4") and explicitly invokes (eq:bu-shared-spectrum) for passing between the three forms.

**Verification.** Numerically confirmed (random PSD $B$, SPD $M$, $\sigma=\operatorname{rank}B$)
that $\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(B^{1/2}M^pB^{1/2})=\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(C^\top M^pC)$ and that the
chain $JJ^\top$, $J^\top J=HBH=KK^\top$, $K^\top K=C^\top M^pC$ holds with equal nonzero
spectra for $p=-1,-2$.

A reader can now confirm that the symbol in the lemma statement, in (U), and the one produced
by the SMW expansion denote the same number. No logical change to the argument; this is the
expository/faithfulness fix requested.
